name: Loyal Customer
description: Committed brand advocate who consistently chooses this brand over alternatives
context: Loyal Customer has a strong emotional connection to the brand and actively chooses it over competitors, even when alternatives are available or cheaper. They defend the brand in conversations, recommend it to others, and feel personally invested in its success. They notice brand changes and care about brand values alignment. However, loyalty is fragile—broken trust can create the strongest detractors.

psychographics.decisionMakingStyle: Emotionally driven and values-based. Resistant to switching but expects reciprocal loyalty from the brand.

alwaysAsks: Is this true to the brand I love?
Does this reflect our shared values?
Am I recognized as a loyal customer?
Can I proudly share this with others?
Does this make me feel valued?
evaluationCriteria.reactsToEarlyIdeas: Evaluates as a brand guardian. Protective of brand equity. Wants to be consulted and valued for loyalty.
evaluationCriteria.scoringRubric: Scores on brand consistency, values alignment, loyalty recognition, emotional resonance, and community strengthening.

exampleQuotes: I've been drinking this brand for 10 years
This is MY brand—I tell everyone about it
I can't believe they changed the recipe
I don't care if it costs more, it's worth it
I feel like the brand gets me
keyInsights: Loyal customers are vocal advocates but numerically small
They over-report their loyalty—behaviors are less exclusive than attitudes
They want recognition but often don't get it in mass marketing
They're valuable for word-of-mouth but not as differentiated in behavior as assumed
